entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Server 2020 TRIAL - Is Business View Mana...
entry.content:
Hello,

One of the functions of the latest CRS or BOE is the Client tools cannot be installed on the same PC as the Main server, it causes conflicts.

Or you can try installing them on the same PC as your Server install.

What you need to do is install the CRS Client Tools on a different PC to be able to access the BV app. You can access the Client tool  Install from the same installer as the CRS install.

For more details and help files go here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESSOBJECTS_BUSINESS_INTELLIGENCE_PLATFORM?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: block outbound delivery creation based on cofirmed de...
entry.content: the process now only suggests the days of delivery in the unloading point. this is acceptable

entry.title: Re: Issues in Cost Centers: Actual/Plan/Variance report
entry.content:
Hello

please check COFC tcode, there must be records for activities.

if COFC error is for missing price, fill kp26 with price, the run cofc and clear it (save)

after that the activities will be shown in 611 and ksii report 

( i guess you had used bapi or z program for production)

best regards 

Ghazal Afshar

entry.title: RAP / Restful Application Programming Mode: ListReport on ObjectPage but restrict further navigation
entry.content:
Hello @ALL,

the MDE of a given CDS Root View Entity consists of multiple facets, some of these are of the type #FIELDGROUP_REFERENCE (for purpose: #HEADER), some have the type #LINEITEM_REFERENCE (for purpose: #STANDARD).

A lineitem facet is intended to be displayed on the object page, as such it utilizes the annotation 'targetElement' with reference to a composition[0..*] _Example. The data is successfully pulled into the facet and the object page correctly displays the data being present.

But there is a problem.

Generally, the definition of lineitems automatically allows for further navigation - so if a lineitem is clicked on the initial page of a list report for example, then navigation to the object page behind it will start. This will also happen, if the lineitems have been defined inside the object page - such that - a lineitem clicked on the object page, will navigate further into the next page.

Is it possible to define a list of items on the object page, which do not allow further navigation, but could possibly be edited if necessary via the appropiate CRUD operation?

The goal is to have a list displayed like:

In the image example above, it appears as though further navigation would not be possible by clicking on one of its items. This restriction is activily sought for in my scenario, but I can not find any sources with regards to its implementation.

Thanks in advance for every comment!

entry.title: One Plant multiple warehouse under one plant
entry.content:
I have seen several scenarios for multiple plants with one warehouse.

This scenario I need one plant and 4 houses that are on the same street with different addresses

customer only wants 1 plant. 

In CBC when comes to setting the address for the Wh it will automaticly look for the disposable party or you can manually assign. 

Starter system for instance you assign 17W to 17411710 I assume you cannot assign the same DP to different warehouses. 

So my question is do you have to create new disoposible part and if so can you tie them to same plant?

How can I achieve the 4 warehouses so they have their actual shipping and delivery address?

@Christian_Ries

entry.title: Re: Application is crashing after some time while exporti...
entry.content:
First thing you should be doing is keeping up with the CR for VS updates, latest one is SP 37 which you can get from here:

https://origin.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/site/index.html

See my Blog on upgrading:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

The other thing is ask them how much data they are exporting and remind them CR is not a Database Archiving tool, they can use the Database Server to do that.

Likely they are attempting to export too much data and the PDF file is too big.

To debug try exporting to some other format.

Try exporting less data, a simple one or two page report for instance to see if that works.

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 Fragment Dialog with Fixed Toolbar and Scroll...
entry.content:
Hi Santosh, 

Is this what you would like to achieve?

https://plnkr.co/edit/AOpIfKMVX23izvkn?preview

 

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:core="sap.ui.core">
<Dialog id="idDialog" title="Sample Dialog" stretch="false" resizable="true">
 

<VBox fitContainer="true">
<VBox id="VB1">
<RadioButtonGroup id="idRBG1">
<RadioButton id="idRB1" text="Option 1"/>
<RadioButton id="idRB2" text="Option 2"/>
</RadioButtonGroup>
</VBox>
</VBox>

 
<ScrollContainer height="300px" vertical="true">
<VBox id="VB2">
<Table id="idTable" items="{demoModel>/TableItems}" sticky="ColumnHeaders">
<columns>
<Column>
<Text text="ID"/>
</Column>
<Column>
<Text text="Name"/>
</Column>
<Column>
<Text text="Category"/>
</Column>
</columns>

<items>
<ColumnListItem>
<cells>
<Text text="{demoModel>id}"/>
<Text text="{demoModel>name}"/>
<Text text="{demoModel>category}"/>
</cells>
</ColumnListItem>
</items>
</Table>
</VBox>
</ScrollContainer>

<buttons>
<Button id="idB1" text="Action 1" press="onAction1"/>
<Button id="idB2" text="Action 2" press="onAction2"/>
<Button id="idClose" text="Close" press="onCloseDialog"/>
</buttons>

</Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>
 
Regards
Virinchy

entry.title: Re: PCR for OT calculations
entry.content:
Create a "Counter" Time Type to count the Overtime hours worked in the week.  On the first day of the week, that counter is set back to 0.00 at the beginning of the Time Evaluation Schema.

On Scheduled Work Days, Overtime hours can be established using the Hours Worked and the Work Schedule Hours.  On Days OFF, all hours are Overtime.

Each day (except on the first day of the week), look at Yesterday's value of t"Counter" and establish if Today's Overtime Hours fall in the 1,5x or 2.0x category, and in what proportion.

entry.title: Re: Limitations in Capacity Planning in SAP S/4HANA Publi...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, traditional CM25 (Capacity Planning Table) and CM27
(Capacity Scheduling Board) transactions are not available. Instead, capacity planning is handled via
Fiori apps like "Capacity Scheduling" and "Manage Work Center Capacity."

Key Limitations:
1. No Classic Capacity Planning Transactions – CM25/CM27 are not supported.
2. Limited Customization – Public Cloud restricts deep customization compared to On-Premise.
3. Live Cache Dependency – Live Cache is used in PP/DS (Production Planning & Detailed Scheduling) but may not fully replace CM25/CM27 functionalities.
4. Alternative Apps – Use Fiori-based capacity planning tools with embedded analytics.

entry.title: Dynamic Breaks
entry.content:
We have a requirement that if an employee works 8.5 hours, apply a 30-minute break or if an employee works 9 hours, apply a 1-hour break.  We configured the Dynamic Break as follows.  The problem is it is recording 30 minutes after 8.5 and another hour after 9 hours.  It should be recording only one or the other.  So, if I worked 9 hours, I receive a 1-hour break only. 

 

Dynamic Break
Working Time (hh:mm) Accumulated Break in Minutes External Code Entity UUID
08:30  
30  
1  
82C04AE25E7142039C10B1B38B98C145
09:00  
60  
2  
E4E89981517A4812B4FE1EF2C6622E33

entry.title: Re: VB6 application with Crystal report
entry.content:
First issue is VB 6 is no longer support by Microsoft and SAP CR products follow MS's end of life cycle.

Time to upgrade to CR for VS .NET which you can get from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

There is 2 sample app's on that page as well to get you started.

That error typically indicates the DB Client is not configured or doesn't exist on the PC. Reports save the client dll name in the RPT file and looks for it when running the reports in an application.

Likely possibility is they may be using a current OS which is definitely not supported in old legacy applications.

Good luck

entry.title: Re: Exploring the SAP HANA XS Advanced Secure Store functionality with NodeJS
entry.content:
Dear Luis,

I created a test user by command:

call SYS.USER_SECURESTORE_INSERT ('TestStoreName', False, 'TestKey','ab2467cdef');

You can find a list of the application users in view:

SELECT * FROM APPLICATION_ENCRYPTION_KEYS WHERE COMPONENT = 'SqlSecureStore';

and a more detailed in view:

SELECT * FROM SYS.P_DPAPI_KEY_ WHERE CALLER = 'SqlSecureStore';

 

For additional information refer to

XSA Maintain Values in the SAP HANA Secure Store

Cloud Foundry: Maintain Values in the SAP HANA Secure Store

Best regards

Axel

entry.title: Consuming components in production
entry.content:
I would like to know what are the differences between the functionalities offered by the On-Premise and Cloud solution. Specifically, in the EWM module. And, more specifically, the differences in how the PP and EWM modules are integrated, so it refers to the functionality of consuming components in production orders that have been made available (Staging) in EWM.

So, for example, in the Cloud version, I have discovered that I can consume directly from the ERP - a WT is created in EWM - specifically, from the equivalent of tx. CO27.

However, from the On-Premise version, it always generates me an ODO. is this so, or is there a way also in On-Premise to consume directly?

entry.title: SAP Tech Talks Community Events by EPAM
entry.content:
Hello SAP  Community in Yerevan!

I would like to invite you to join our Tech Talks community by EPAM. your SAP journey's perfect companion. Whether you're new to SAP or an experienced pro, we're your one-stop source for SAP insights.

What We Offer:

✅ Stay Informed: Get the latest IT news, trends, and expert insights.

✅ Engage: Connect with SAP enthusiasts and experts.

✅ SAP Events: Stay updated on SAP events and webinars.

✅ Best Practices: Learn from the best to excel in SAP technologies.

Join us to fuel innovation and proficiency in SAP technologies. Let's explore together!

I would like to bring your attention specifically to the latest event on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Pricing App designed to provide insights, strategies, and best practices for leveraging the SAP BTP to its full potential.

Whether you are looking to optimize your current SAP setup or considering adopting the SAP BTP, this webinar is a valuable opportunity to learn directly from the experts.

For further details and to register, please visit SAP BTP Pricing App Event. This is an opportunity you won't want to miss as you continue your SAP journey.

We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: Re: 1 job requisition for multiple identical positions
entry.content:
Dear @Preij ,

You will be pleased to know that in SuccessFactors Recruiting, you can use 1 Requisition to be tagged to and fill multiple Positions. I always recommend and setup this solution for my clients and have been tremendously welcomed, e.g. consulting firms who have evergreen and bulk hires for Audit Associates, or healthcare industry who have bulk hires for healthcare workers.

Prerequisites:

- In the Requisition template, you must use the standard Position object field, plus define it as "multiselect" (this itself is the key)

- In your Offer Approval, define the standard Position object field as a mandatory field

The outcome and process for this:

Let's suppose the 10 Analyst positions are identical or near-identical, better still they are reporting to the same Hiring Manager

1. Let Hiring Manager create the 1st Requisition from 1 Position first. Position-Requisition integration will ensure the necessary Position details gets populated to this 1 Requisition.

2. Let Hiring Manager state in the Requisition that he/she needs other reporting positions to be filled as well because they are identical positions (10 Analyst openings to be filled)

3. When the Requisition is routed to the Recruiter, let the Recruiter tag additional Positions to the same Requisition. 10 Analyst positions are now tagged to this 1 Requisition. Increase the number of opening to 10. Reserve these edit permissions to only the Recruiter.

4. Recruiter post this 1 Requisition and look out for 10 suitable Analyst candidates. 

5. When it comes to Offer Approval, the standard position object will listed out the 10 Positions tagged in Requisition. It should be a mandatory field such that Recruiter needs to state which of the 10 Positions each of the 10 selected Analyst candidates needs to be hired into. This is crucial to be done before initiating onboarding. Recruiter must tag 1 selected Candidate to 1 Position at this point in time.

6. After initiating onboarding, since 1 candidate is already assigned to 1 position, there will not be any issues for the same Recruiter or Onboarding team to process the candidate through onboarding and manage pending hires. 

7. Recruiter can keep the 1 Requisition posted and use it as an Applicant Pool for future Analyst positions for the same Manager. As the Positions are filled, Recruiter can untag those Positions from the Requisition. As new identical Positions to be hired come up, Recruiter can tag them to the Requisition which already accumulated the Applicant Pool! Then, just keep increasing the Number of Openings accordingly as new Positions at tagged. Do not reduce it as the candidates parked in Hired status will still count towards the Number of Openings filled.

8. Recruiter can decide how long they want to keep this Requisition as an Applicant Pool, example, at max 6 months or 1 year, depending on the applicants accumulated. This is because the applicant count can increase to the thousands and even more. Also, the applicant pool can become "old". At certain points in time, the Requisition becomes hard to operate and it is better to start afresh in a new requisition.

I always recommend this approach and implement this solution to my clients who are frequently concerned with having bulk or evergreen recruitments for identical positions. For example, a Recruiter for a Consulting Firm cannot be posting 200 Requisitions for the same Audit Associate position or a Recruiter for a Hospital cannot be posting 80 Requisitions for Nurses in the same department.

However, Recruiters should be aware to use 1 Requisition for only identical/near identical Positions, otherwise it will be hard for Recruiters to manage. Keep in mind that:

- 1 Requisition means 1 set of Job Description posted; hence the Description only attracts the candidates that it is intended to

- 1 Requisition means it stores only 1 set of Position details, i.e. same Department, Division, etc. 

- 1 Requisition means it stores only 1 Hiring Manager, that flows to Onboarding

Hope this helps!

Best Regards
Ernest Lin

entry.title: Re: When running Query in Analysis for Microsoft Office user get error - [3ccb-6ef7-ee] Nested excep
entry.content: did you check Analysis-configuration? did you check 32 or 64bit Office-Installation? did you check firewall/remote-access diff. between the users?

entry.title: New Perspectives From An Elder Community Member
entry.content:
In 2025, I volunteered to continue community service in the SAP "Ecosystem." Why, you might ask, as I have no access to any working SAP system other than the community. Part stubbornness, part "keep contributing", and part because many (but not all) of my global tech contacts started here. And in this world, we plan to get around.

A year ago, the SAP Community site was migrated from multiple prior systems to the current landscape. This monumental shift was preceded by announced plans to adjust content to the new design, testing, and user feedback. Some content was planned for removal for various reasons, which ended up less dramatic than anticipated, though some treasured moments are lost in time ("like tears in rain").

Time Settings

I chose to set the "time/date" display field to absolute dates as shown above, with a push-the-envelope value of "ddd" for the day of the month. Yes, months don't go above 99, so I get a leading 0 which tells me I am logged in since my settings work.

Good

All change, especially in web forms, brings challenges which people manage in a spectrum ranging from leading the wave, sitting in the middle, or trying to drag or object. The best result of the Community platform of having a coherent/cohesive set of controls was less visible to casual users who might not have recognized a WordPress blog post from a BlogSpot from a Medium. 

The history retention, keeping posts back to the early 2000's, enables those who might be gifted an old but running system to be migrated or archived, some clues, if they can set their search parameters correctly. I'm thinking the internal SAP site search will continue to improve over legacy external search sites that seem to bent on pushing quick fixes over useful results.

Other than the yet-to-be fixed bug with extraneous new-lines, I'm satisfied with the post editor for how I draft and share content. Yes, those familiar with the WordPress tool-kit are dismayed at the differences, to which I think of the maxim "it's a poor worker who blames their tool" for the results. I do miss the "you have 500 words here" feedback because one way I track my effort is number of words, which sort-of translates to reading comprehension time. I don't want to skimp on words, nor do I want to run on and one.

The rearrangement of the prior landscape to new spaces and categories is generally a success in my view, in particular separating posts by SAP staff from the rest of us hoi polloi. For this "year-in-review" post I chose the Welcome Corner with the idea that both new members and veterans will find this instructive.

Not-Quite-Good

In the hey-day of SCN, flurries of activities whirled around the recognition system, where members gained points for adding content, and could compare one group against another to demonstrate skills (real or imagined) in solving roll-out, ramp-up, or cool-down obstacles. "Cheaters" were dealt with via moderation, and "Rock Stars" were given platforms to continue contributing. The new platform has potential for both awards and consequences, and both sides need care and feeding. A common error is asking a question as a part of a prior thread (which is also common on other Q&A sites); some of our attempts to correct this behavior have worked, some not. Badges and contests have slowed to a trickle in 2025.

The changes from having blog post authority to needing approval in certain spaces was inevitable, I presume, but has caused some to move to other places or stop contributing. I've had delays and other obstacles that might have driven off someone less motivated to contribute. The mothballing ("sunset") of the Information Architecture space was poorly communicated and executed, in my opinion. I can understand if an entity does a reorg, or adds/removes products from the portfolio. But I had a draft ready to publish when I learned that area was being removed. Or maybe being set read-only. Or, what.

Going to a Universal ID was another change that somewhat coincided with the platform change, and for most members was not a big deal. For others, though, losing access or privileges was a diversion from contributing which might result in simply giving up. Over a year later, I still run into inexplicable "Authentication Unsuccessful" messages and unpredictable timeouts.

Needs Work

Among the faults in the current platform is the lack of a central content view per member. If you only stay in one topic space that's not an issue but is you stray into multiple areas you may be unable to find a draft you started then didn't finish or submit for approval. In this space ("Welcome Corner Blog Posts"), I could see the draft of this content, but I also see 2 other alleged drafts.

Drafts + 2 not-drafts

The content from 2018 is not really a draft despite the title, and I would likely never have noticed until I started new content (the assignment of prior content to current spaces was done by an algorithm).

There seem to be undeveloped areas of recognition and identity, unsurprising given the constant rise-and-fall of social media sites one might wish to share. Adding a custom footer with your signature is nice, and cool [see https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/new-sap-community-profile-signatures/ba-p/13598678] but does seem like a clunky afterthought.

Lastly, the prevalence of spam posts over the past year has been a battle. Because the site needs to allow new users to join, there seemed no way to prevent infiltration of scummy content like money-services. As they targeted larger groups, the noise became unbearable, causing me to un-subscribe from notifications. Which then caused me to think less about posting here (out of sight, out of mind). Kudos to those in the Community office repelling the boarders and keeping the content from being overwhelmed with irrelevancies or worse.

Final deep thoughts

I have not mothballed the ABAP Detective series quite yet. Keep circulating the tapes.

entry.title: Re: You have reached your upload limit of 1,000 images.
ScopeItem:BKP Storage location suggestions when returning to customer(Send Back to Customer)
entry.content:
Hi Expet！

I would like to use ScopeItem:BKP to achieve the following.

①A customer has returned a product and the returned product has been placed into storage location 151R.
　Sales Order Type: Register the Sales Order with CBAR.
　The parameters are as follows:
　・Follow-up: 0001 Receive into Plant
　・Next Follow-up: 0021 Send Back to Customet
　Select 151R as the Storage Location for the Return Sales Order.

② Move product from Storage Location 151R to Storage Location 151A.

③ Create the Outbound Delivery for the return to the customer using the app "Enter Inspection Resluts".
　When creating a Outbound Delivery, I want to ship from Storage Location 151A, not from Storage Location 　151R.

 

Please tell me how to achieve this.

Soma

entry.title: Event onSelectionChanged from InteractiveDonutChart doesn't work
entry.content:
Hi,

I don't understand why the event onSelectionChanged from the class 

sap.suite.ui.microchart.InteractiveDonutChart isn't called when I click on any segment.
It's been called within a custom section, in a fiori element application.
The method onSectionLoaded is correctly called, but  onSelectionChanged don't. 
When I run the application, the console doesn't bring any errors, neither in the network tab.
 

 

<core:FragmentDefinition 
	xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" 
	xmlns="sap.m"
	xmlns:microchart="sap.suite.ui.microchart" >

	<HBox>

		<microchart:InteractiveDonutChart
			id="idDonutChart"
			selectionChanged="onSelectionChanged"
			press="onSelectionChanged"
			selectionEnabled="true"
			displayedSegments="8"
			segments="{_lgort}">
			<microchart:segments>
				<microchart:InteractiveDonutChartSegment
					value="{QuantityStock}"
					label="{= ${StorageLocation} + ' - ' + ${StorageLocationTxt} }" />
			</microchart:segments>
		</microchart:InteractiveDonutChart>

	</HBox>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

 

 

 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageToast"
], function(MessageToast) {
    'use strict';

    return {
        onPress: function(oEvent) {
            MessageToast.show("onPress");
            debugger;
        },

        onInit: function(oEvent){
            MessageToast.show("onInit");
            debugger;
        },

        onSectionLoaded: function(oSection){
            MessageToast.show("onSectionLoaded");
            debugger;
        },

        onSelectionChanged: function (oSection) {
            debugger;
		},

        press: function(oEvent){
            debugger;
        }
    };
});

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Key User Extensibility – Setting up associations within Custo

entry.title: Re: Hi Everyone!
entry.content: Welcome Razia to the SAP Community. Keep sharing your knowledge

entry.title: How to Calculate/Filter Aggregation of Measures for different hierarchy subtotals in SAC/HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

It is a long post but I want to give all details to explain clearly what we try to achieve.

We are preparing a sales report that contains a three-level category hierarchy.

  • Data Source: HANA Cloud Calculation View
  • Connection Type: Live Connection
  • Hierarchy Type: Level-Based Hierarchy
  • Story Type: Optimized Story
  • Component Type to Represent Data in the Story: Table, KPI Tile (Numeric Point)

Context

To briefly explain the context, consider the sales of a retail store where multiple products from different categories are sold within a single transaction (check). Our objective is to analyze:

  • The sales quantity and amount of these products, and
  • The number of checks in which these products appear, along with the total sales amount of those checks.

We have four main measures:

  1. Number of Items Sold: Represents the number of products sold. 
  2. Number of Checks: Shows the number of distinct checks (transactions) in which a specific product has been sold.
  3. Sales Amount of the Item: Displays the total sales amount of a product across different checks.
  4. Total Sales Amount of Checks: Displays the total amount of the checks that include that specific product. – WE HAVE A PROBLEM WITH THIS MEASURE
    • (According to the example in the screenshot, product P001 has been sold in checks 1001, 1002, and 1003. This measure sums the total sales amounts of these three checks, including all other products sold in those transactions. You can analyze this further in the attached screenshot )

The Challenge/Problem

The total sales amount of items sold and the total sales amount of the checks containing these products must match at the total row.

Since same check may contain different products, the challenge arises when displaying the data in a table:

  • The Total Sales Amount of Checks column gets summed across multiple occurrences, causing double counting. If different products are in the same check, since the table contains product level data, this measure shows total sales amount of that check in 2 different row and sums these values at total node.

What possible solutions can be implemented in HANA Cloud Calculation View or SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to handle this requirement correctly?

Looking forward to your support. Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP Build: Create Business Apps, Processes and website without Code(Chennai, India)
entry.content: Hi @Rekha_DR Mam , Please share the PPT of today's session.

例如：會計主管可以執行[過帳]，但會計人員不能執行。

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Enhancements - February 2025
entry.content: Apologies if I’m posting this in the wrong place. I’ve noticed that the title in the notification feed is often truncated. Would it be possible to display the full text within the notification or, at the very least, show the complete title when hovering over the title, potentially as an improvement for future releases? This would make it easier to understand the notification's content without needing to open it. Thank you!

entry.title: Missing "Profit center in Manage customer line items"
entry.content:
We are very frequently receiving queries these days from business on this topic that when an an AR journal entry has been posted with a "Profit center" than why that profit center is not visible in "Manage customer line items" ? however when we check the same journal entry in "Display line items in general ledger we can see the profit center ? Screenshots attached for your reference. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Missing "Profit center in Manage customer line items"
entry.content: See blog How to Display Profit Center Assignments for AR/AP... - SAP Community

entry.title: Migrate from Sybase PowerBuilder to S/4H ?
entry.content: I have a customer with a large Sybase PowerBuilder code base. They're flagging this a maintenance/supportability risk and proposing a complete re-write. Does SAP offer a standard tool /guidance for helping migrate away ? Thanks

entry.title: Re: Maintaining Payment Model in EC to ECP
entry.content: You'll have to use the BAdI for P0014 to read the payment model using FM "HR_MODEL_PROPOSE".  If this FM returns a payment model, you'll then need to clear the following fields on P0014 as they can't be used along with a payment model as it's one or the other:  ZDATE, ZFPER, ZANZL and ZEINZ.

entry.title: Select maximun and minimum date/time of a table
entry.content:
Hi.

I have a custom table with several fields and two of them are Date and Time.

I need to see the minimum date/time (start datetime) and the maximum date/time (end datetime).

How could I do this, date and time are in separated fields.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: How to clean a GRC obsolete data (Access Requests, EAM Logs, Owners, etc)
entry.content:
Hello there everyone, we are planning to create a new Client for one of our divisions here and we would like to know how can we clean it up since the SAP_ALL was one of the only option of profile we had to bring the standards and customization we have made and it also brought access requests, logs, and everything else that will not be useful for our new users.

Is there any best practice on that kind of "clean up"?

entry.title: Re: Enhancing CDS View with a field from Custom Business Object - S4 cloud

entry.title: CQN subquery not working for local/external services in CAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm using CAP(Node.js) with odata(v4) & fiori elements. I'm trying to use CQN subqueries and execute them at service level i.e. using srv.run(query) , srv here is a service object created by cds.connect.to('ServiceName'). According to Capire documentation you can use a query as source in the from property : https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/cds/cqn#from

This works but only when you run the query using cds.run(query). My requirement is to execute the query in other services i.e. srv.run(). But when i try doing that it gives the following error : [cds] - TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map')

And when you click on the file( resolveView.js i.e. an internal file) that the error is referring to, it shows the following code , error is on line 653: 

 Seems like it expects an object with ref property as key and an array as value i.e from = {ref : ['TableName']}, and works only if the source is a table & not another query. Is this the default behavior and is there any way around it ?

entry.title: Re: DIALOG TO CALL OOALV TWICE AND OOALV SHOW WRONG DATA
entry.content: HI,ulrich_mhrke. I have added my code attachment. My cl_gui_container is a local variable, and I do not need to release it.

entry.title: Re: Sales KIT

Hello Sir,

We are going to explore the variant combination to see if is possible to select a combination from a sales bom in a sale order.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to activate the OData service in next system(QA/PRD) in BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content: hi @kirankumar_angar, Thanks for the response. When we publish the service in ADT, it is published locally and it is not captured in the TR. In the On-Prem world we have an option to tackle this easily. My question is on BTP ABAP do we need to manually activate the service in each system (Similar to how we are doing BTP ABAP Dev system or is there a way to transport this, if yes How?) ..Thanks.

entry.title: Warum Change Management bei einer SAP Cloud Migration wichtig ist - ein Blick in die Praxis
entry.content:
In der aktuellen Folge des EducationNewsCast Podcast diskutierte ich mit Philipp Blaesing von Siemens Healthineers und Anja Scheurer von SAP die entscheidende Rolle des Change Managements bei SAP Cloud Migrationen, insbesondere im Rahmen des RISE-Programms. Hier könnt ihr reinhören auf 🎧 SAP Podcasts - Apple - Spotify. Anbei eine Zusammenfassung als Blog. 

Das RISE-Projekt bei Siemens Healthineers: Eine globale IT-Initiative

Das RISE-Projekt bei Siemens Healthineers ist eine der größten IT-Initiativen des Unternehmens. Es umfasst die Migration von über 20 SAP-Systemen und angrenzenden Applikationen in die SAP Private Cloud.

Philipp Blaesing betont: "Innovation hat bei uns einen hohen Stellenwert bei Siemens Healthineers. Und da in Zukunft alle Innovationen in der RISE Cloud entwickelt werden, hat natürlich auch die Migration bei uns eine sehr hohe Visibility und Wichtigkeit."

Technische Migration vs. Geschäftsprozess-Transformation: Ein zweistufiger Ansatz

Ein interessanter Aspekt des Projekts ist die Trennung von technischer Migration und Geschäftsprozess-Transformation. Blaesing erklärt: "Wir trennen die technische Migration von den Business-Transformationsprojekten. [...] Erst ein stabiles Fundament gießen, wenn man mal so eine Metapher von einem Hausbau sieht und dann kann man die Räume draufbauen, wie man sie will."

Obwohl es sich primär um ein technisches Projekt handelt, unterstreicht Anja Scheurer die Bedeutung des Change Managements:

"Auch wenn RISE-Projekte auf den ersten Blick eben nur als ein technisches Projekt scheinen, betreffen technologische Veränderungen, technologische Transformationen immer die Menschen, die sie dann schlussendlich auch natürlich anwenden müssen."

Die Rolle des Change Managements in technischen Migrationsprojekten

Die Zusammenarbeit zwischen Projekt- und Change Management ist eng verzahnt. Anja Scheurer erläutert: "Grundsätzlich arbeiten wir eng auf Programmebene, sowohl aber auch eben auf der Projektebene mit den jeweiligen Verantwortlichen zusammen." Das Change Management-Team fungiert als Cross-Workstream im Programm und gibt die Methodik und den Change- und User-Kommunikationsplan auf Programmebene vor.

Die Verbindung von Change- und Projekt Management 

Change Management und Projektmanagement waren bei der SAP Cloud Migration von Siemens Healthineers eng verzahnt:

  • Integration: Change Management als Cross-Workstream im Gesamtprogramm.
  • Klare Rollen: Deutliche Aufgabenteilung zwischen PMO und Change Team.
  • Operative Einbindung: Change-Experten in Projektterminen und Cutover-Meetings.
  • Kommunikation: Während das PMO die interne Programmkommunikation vorantrieb, fokussierte sich das Change Management Team auf die Nutzer- und Nutzenkommunikation.
  • Ressourcenbereitstellung: Das Change Management Team stellte den Projektleitern Werkzeuge und Materialien zur Verfügung, um Change-Aktivitäten umzusetzen.

Diese enge Zusammenarbeit ermöglichte eine durchgängige Berücksichtigung von Change-Aspekten und schnelle Reaktionen auf Herausforderungen im Projektverlauf. 

Bewährte Methoden und Tools im Change Management

Bei den eingesetzten Methoden und Tools hat sich das Team anfangs am ADKAR-Modell orientiert, dieses aber an die spezifischen Bedürfnisse des Projekts angepasst. Bewährt haben sich klassische Stakeholder-Analysen, Engagement-Pläne und Change-Readiness-Checks. Blaesing betont auch die Wichtigkeit eines guten People-Networks und vielfältiger Kommunikationskanäle.

Learnings und Tipps für erfolgreiche SAP Migrationsprojekte

Als wichtigste Lernerfahrung nennt Philipp Blaesing die frühzeitige Einbindung des Change Managements:

".. auf jeden Fall das Change Management so früh wie möglich zu involvieren, also schon in den Planungsphasen." Anja Scheurer ergänzt: "Unterschätzt den Bedarf einfach überhaupt nicht für das Change Management und der Kommunikation in Migrationsprojekten, bindet eben die Kollegen und Kolleginnen früh ein."

Auch gaben beide konkrete Tipps für die Praxis:

  • Eine klare RACI-Matrix für Change- und Kommunikations-verantwortlichkeiten erstellen
  • Vielfältige Kommunikationskanäle nutzen (Newsletter, Webseiten, Q&A-Sessions ... )
  • Regelmäßige Feedback-Runden mit Stakeholdern durchführen
  • Change Management als offiziellen Cross-Workstream im Programm etablieren

Inspirierende Aussagen aus dem Podcast

"Wenn man das Gefühl hat, man kommuniziert zu viel, dann war es richtig."
"Change Management ist sehr viel mehr in den Mittelpunkt gerückt als vor fünf bis zehn Jahren und gewinnt immer mehr an Wichtigkeit."

Resümee

Die Erfahrungen von Siemens Healthineers zeigen, dass eine frühzeitige Einbindung, klare Strukturen und vielfältige Kommunikation entscheidend sind. 

Was ich wichtig finde ist auch folgendes: Change Management erweist sich auch bei primär technischen Projekten als wichtiger Erfolgsfaktor. Die Handlungsfelder sind vielleicht etwas anders. So gibt es beispielsweise wenig End-User Training. Aber Änderungen die Prozesse, Jobs und Aufgaben betreffen gibt es auch bei technischen Projekten. Und auch Fragen zum Mehrwert eines solchen technischen Projekts und die Besprechung und Kommunikation dieser Vorteile. Und hier ist eben Change Management sehr wichtig mit all seinen Facetten, von Stakeholder Analysen bis Kommunikation

⁉️ Habt ihr weitere Beispiele, was bei sehr technischen Projekten besonders ist rund um Change Management? Freue mich auf Kommentare hier auch. 

Shownotes

Buch: Die 5 Sprachen der Mitarbeitermotivation

Buch: Der Change Code von Dieter Lederer

Doku der Community-Session mit Anja zum SAP Change Starterkit

SAP News: Siemens Healthineers entscheidet sich für RISE with SAP um Leistung zu steigern

Aus-und Weiterbildung bei Siemens Healthineers

entry.title: Re: Error WARN in Item Connector
entry.content: Is there a transform data rule to accept "ACTIVO" as the status value? As per the Course (Item) Data Connector File Columns, the values would be "N" and "Y".

entry.title: Register Now – Q1 EC User Group Meeting – Mar 18th
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the "Register Now" button or "Yes" to "Will you be attending?" will NOT register you for the event. To register for the event, please REGISTER HERE to receive the meeting invite and login details. 

Q1 Employee Central User Group Meeting

Tuesday, March 18th – 11:00 am - 12:30 pm Eastern

REGISTER NOW!

Our Employee Central User Group is customer led by Corning and PepsiCo. This meeting will provide you with an opportunity to connect with peer Employee Central customers, submit questions to chat with others about, hear from EC experts and listen to a Customer Spotlight.

If you haven’t already, please subscribe to the Customer Empowerment Events Newsletter HERE!  It’s a great resource to stay up to date on all User Group sessions globally.  Also, please check out the User Group Calendar HERE!

Any questions about this meeting, please reach out to Lindsay Westra, Director, User Engagement, SAP SuccessFactors at lindsay.westra@sap.com.  Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Ignore previous completion flag Completion

entry.title: Re: Voice Commands in SAP Asset Manager
entry.content:
You can use nativescript speech plugin to convert voice to text and then create a deep link to do the navigation. It will be a custom implementation.

Nativescript Speech plugin - https://market.nativescript.org/plugins/nativescript-speech-recognition/

For deep link, you can check DeepLinkLibrary.js

entry.title: Re: New Hire missing in Onboarding Dashboard
entry.content: Hi, have you found a solution? I'm facing similar issue - we added onboardee manually through Add New Recruit to Onboarding and probably because of it now we have "New Recruits (Infinity)", no errors in Business Process, no jobs in BPE, I'm also in BPE Service and SAP Error Flows and there is no info. Through Data Inspector I can see that this person has been assigned to proper manager, email has been sent but dashboard is not ok

entry.title: Re: Importing Price Lists and Volume Pricing
entry.content:
Hi bbranco ,

I used it a long time back so do not know the exact details.

The following is mentioned:

  • CardCode: BP master code or an asterisk followed by the price list number (e.g. *1) only when importing Period and Volume Discounts in cases in which no business partner is defined.

You can test it by putting no value for BP.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: create a new additional product description for different languages in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
I would like to know if there is any way to create a new additional product description for many languages using sap app

 

I tried to use mass update feature but it allows only to update product description in the product master data and not to create a new one. Since there are more than thousands of products, we can not do it manually. Could you please let us know if it possible to create it directly via migration template or with some app?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: create a new additional product description for diffe...
entry.content:
The Migration Object 'Product - extend existing record by new org levels' allows to create new descriptions for a Product

 

Please check the resource Product - extend existing record by new org levels  : 'You can also maintain new descriptions, new units of measure, and new GTINs.'

 

The Mass update functionality included in 'Mass Maintenance for Master Data' allows also creation as well as change using the _ACTION_CODE" = “I” outlined at Specific Syntax for Dynamic File Upload

entry.title: Re: BusinessObjects Query builder queries - Part IV
entry.content: Amazing

entry.title: SSO Error in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need your help.

I have configured IAS as a proxy, and in IAS, I have set the Subject Name Identifier=employee_id

In Azure (OpenID Connection to IAS), I have added the employee_id to Additional Claims.

In the source system (Azure) in IPS, I have configured the transformation as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.employeeId",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
}

Also in IPS transformation I replaced "sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName" with "sourcePath": "$.employeeId".

Users are provisioned with IPS.

In IAS, the "Login Name" field for users receives the employee_id, for example, 1111. These users are then provisioned to SAC and have the employee_id (1111) in the "USER ID" field as well.

When I try to test SSO and select "Choose a user attribute to map to your Identity Provider" -> User attribute set = "USER ID", the user is not found in SAC.

The user accesses the SAC link and enters their email address. The user is authenticated in Azure, but in SAC, the user is not found.

I need help.

I can't solve this by myself.

Thank you very much !!!

entry.title: Dynamic Text in Column Headers of Table Section in SAC
entry.content:
Dear All 

this is my problem please suggest how can I manually add year irrespective of measure dimensions coming from a model You are working on a live data model in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), fetching data from SAP BW. In your data, you have a dimension called "Category," which includes members like "Outlook," "Business Plan 2025," "Business Plan 2026," "Business Plan 2027," "Business Plan 2028," and "Business Plan 2029." You have created five additional calculated columns derived from these original columns and hidden the original columns. Now, you want to ensure that the column headers dynamically update when the year changes (e.g., from 2025 to 2026). Specifically, you want the column headers to automatically adjust to "Business Plan 2026," "Business Plan 2027," "Business Plan 2028," "Business Plan 2029," and "Business Plan 2030" without manual intervention.

entry.title: EC Time Off accrual calculation: consider Saturday on the full monthe of absences
entry.content:
hi, 

in my company we calculate monthly holiday accruals. If there is absences on the entire month there is no accrual calculation. I used the the function "Get Absence In Days For Period Based On Calendar Days For Time Types()" and it works well. 

How ever, if the absence starts on sunday for example, and Saturday is the first day of the month (and same if Saturday is the last day of the month) and saturday is a non working day (according the work schedule), the entire month should be considered. 

here the rule I cretated: 

How Can I make sur to catch the saturdays (the non working days) in my calculation, as currently only the full month of absences are catched?

Thanks in advance for your advice!

Sevda

entry.title: Re: V51vp - item was not found - process cancelled during...
entry.content: Yes , batch split is working fine in other systems only in upgraded system it is throwing v51vp item not found error.

entry.title: Why the CFO and the Office of the CFO Should Care About Procurement
entry.content:
In today's dynamic business environment, procurement has evolved from a mere administrative function to a strategic asset that significantly impacts an organization's financial health and sustainability. For Chief Financial Officers (CFOs) and their offices, prioritizing procurement can lead to enhanced financial performance, risk mitigation, and value creation.

 

Enhancing Financial Performance through Procurement

Cost Management and Efficiency

Procurement plays a crucial role in managing both direct and indirect costs. For instance, a multinational conglomerate in the manufacturing industry saves $1 billion annually by localizing its supply chain and negotiating better terms with suppliers. Similarly, a tech giant's procurement team cuts indirect costs by 15% through consolidated purchasing agreements. By effectively managing these costs, CFOs can allocate resources more efficiently and improve the organization's bottom line.

Improving Cash Flow

Effective procurement strategies can improve cash flow by extending payment terms and optimizing inventory levels. For example, a consumer electronics company’s just-in-time procurement model reduces the need for large inventory holdings, freeing up capital for other strategic investments. Improved cash flow management enhances the organization's liquidity position and financial stability, allowing CFOs to invest in growth opportunities.

Enhancing Operational Efficiency

Streamlined procurement processes contribute to overall operational efficiency. Automated procurement systems reduce manual intervention, minimize errors, and speed up the procurement cycle. This efficiency translates into shorter lead times, ensuring that the organization can respond swiftly to market demands and opportunities. Enhancing operational efficiency allows CFOs to drive productivity and reduce operational costs, leading to improved financial performance.

Risk Mitigation

Supply Chain Resilience

The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the vulnerabilities in global supply chains. A diversified supplier base and robust contingency plans help companies maintain operations during disruptions. A resilient supply chain ensures uninterrupted operations, which is critical for maintaining revenue streams and customer satisfaction. By fostering supply chain resilience, CFOs can mitigate operational risks and safeguard the organization's financial stability.

Compliance and Regulatory Adherence

Procurement must adhere to various regulatory requirements, including anti-bribery, anti-corruption, and environmental regulations. Ensuring compliance with these regulations helps avoid legal penalties and reputational damage. Moreover, ethical procurement practices foster trust with stakeholders, enhancing the organization's overall reputation. Ensuring compliance and ethical practices enables CFOs to protect the organization from legal and reputational risks.

Managing Market Volatility

Procurement professionals monitor commodity prices and market trends to anticipate and mitigate the impact of price volatility on the organization's cost structure. By securing long-term contracts and hedging against price fluctuations, procurement helps stabilize costs, allowing CFOs to forecast financial performance more accurately. Managing market volatility supports CFOs in maintaining financial predictability and stability.

Value Creation

Innovation and Competitive Advantage

Strategic procurement drives innovation and creates competitive advantages. Collaborating with suppliers on new product development and process improvements introduces cutting-edge technologies and practices that differentiate the organization in the market. This innovation can lead to new revenue streams and enhanced market positioning. Leveraging procurement for innovation allows CFOs to drive business growth and maintain a competitive edge.

Supplier Relationship Management

Building strong relationships with key suppliers is crucial for long-term success. Effective supplier relationship management (SRM) leads to preferential treatment, early access to new products, and favorable pricing. Prioritizing SRM as a strategic initiative maximizes the value derived from supplier partnerships. Strong supplier relationships enable CFOs to secure better terms and drive cost efficiencies.

Sustainability and Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR)

Sustainable procurement practices align with the organization's CSR goals. By sourcing from environmentally responsible suppliers and promoting fair labor practices, procurement contributes to the organization's sustainability agenda. Emphasizing sustainability in procurement helps CFOs meet CSR objectives and strengthen the organization's reputation.

Data-Driven Decision Making

Leveraging Procurement Analytics

Procurement generates a wealth of data that can inform strategic decision-making. Advanced analytics tools provide insights into spending patterns, supplier performance, and market trends. These insights drive more informed decisions, optimize procurement strategies, and identify opportunities for cost savings and efficiency improvements. Leveraging data analytics allows CFOs to make evidence-based decisions that enhance financial performance.

Integration with Financial Planning

Integrating procurement data with financial planning and analysis (FP&A) processes ensures that procurement activities align with the organization's financial goals. This alignment enables CFOs to create more accurate budgets, forecasts, and financial models, ultimately driving better financial performance. Integrated financial planning supports CFOs in achieving strategic objectives and maximizing value.

Conclusion

Procurement is no longer just an administrative function but a strategic asset for CFOs. By prioritizing procurement, CFOs can achieve cost savings, mitigate risks, and drive innovation. Strategic procurement practices enhance financial performance, ensure compliance, and foster sustainable growth, making it an indispensable part of the CFO's agenda.

entry.title: Re: Using BADI IDOC_DATA_INSERT for populating Z fields in VBAK table from IDOC document
entry.content:
Hello
thanks for the hint
In my understanding this BADI is more to add segments in idoc and not to populate directly a table, am i right ?
On top, can you explain from where you are calling your "fill" method ? Indeed you have created your Z class from se18, you did your own code, but then , how are you calling this code ?

In my case , what i need is : we are receiving inbound idoc orders, so by standard we are passing through the FM IDOC_INPUT_ORDERS. My idea was to have a spot somewhere in an exit in that FM , in which i am calling this BADI implem . Did you do the same ? Or i miss something?

Thanks for the hint

entry.title: Single order process order process
entry.content:
Single order process order process

Single Order:

It is a warehouse process designed to handle individual sales orders efficiently. It ensures that each order is processed separately, optimizing picking, packing, and shipping for high-priority or time-sensitive deliveries. This approach minimizes errors, improves order accuracy, and enhances customer satisfaction by streamlining warehouse operations.

Process Order:
A process order in SAP EWM is used to oversee the execution of production and manufacturing processes within the warehouse. It seamlessly integrates with SAP Production Planning (PP) to coordinate material movements required for production. SAP EWM enhances inventory management and material flow, ensuring efficient handling and optimized warehouse operations based on process order requirements.

Warehouse Task:
A warehouse task in SAP EWM is an operational instruction assigned to workers for executing specific activities such as picking, putaway, or stock transfers. It is automatically generated based on inbound, outbound, or internal warehouse processes, ensuring seamless execution. By systematically managing these tasks, SAP EWM enhances efficiency, accuracy, and productivity in warehouse operations.

Steps for creation of the Single order process order:

Step 1:

Create New Process order using the transaction code CO01

Process order is created.

 

Step 2:

Check the PMR in Transaction /N/SCWM/PMR

Provide the process order and then click on the execute button.

 

Step 3:

Perform Staging in Transaction /N/SCWM/STAGE

Step 4:

Check the Warehouse order linked to Process order

Warehouse order is linked to Process order

Confirm the warehouse order using the confirmation transaction code /n/scwm/to_conf

 

Conclusion:

Single order process order process is provided.

entry.title: Re: IBP Excel Template chart is not working
entry.content:
I have the same issue (in Feb 2025). Simply nothing happens, seems stuck. I updated the SAP_IBP_Chart file but no change. Was there a solution to this?

If we "cancel" the action, it freezes, if I click somewhere on the screen, it shows the following error.

Thanks,

Nandhini

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Elements: Adaptation Project - Adding a Custom Filter to the List Report
entry.content:
Hello Vitor!

This was a great blog and seemed pretty simple to implement. I tried to follow along for an adaptation project I am working on, but I cannot get a new combo box filter to appear. I am working with the Manage Sales Order Fiori app and need to a new filter for Plant to the adapt filter section. I've tried several ways of doing this and yet the combo box does not appear.

This the code I have in the fragment XML file:

The weird this is that the aggregation should be applied to the filterItems aggregation which is deprecated according to the API reference..... So I tried adding the filterGroupItems tag and then I get an error that the combo box does not belong to the type selected show below:

 

Any help with this would be much appreciated!

 

Thanks,

Patrick

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to modify template for Class ICS / C...
entry.content: Using the "SystemEnrollmentNotification" notification template, the "DESCRIPTION:<ESCAPEFORVCAL value="<&VCAL_DESC>"/>" entry was removed from the "VCAL" box. The enrollment email was received, along with the ics (as per the KBA 2375066), and the course ID wasn't in the ics file.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@VickyKoduri it is great to meet you. May I ask where you live? I am in the US outside of Philadelphia. SAP Community offers alot and you may decide to join or run a local event.

Please join what is interesting for you. Best of luck!

Interest Groups | SAP Community

Location Groups | SAP Community

entry.title: Custom Time Dependent Table Date Validations
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I've created a custom time dependent table and generated the table maintenance. In SM30 Table Maintenance, I can see that the table is indeed time dependent as I have the Expand/Collapse and Delimit buttons. However, two things don't behave as expected:

1. I can create two entries with overlapping time frames (and otherwise the same key). In a sap standard time dependent table this is automatically prevented with validation even though I found no events for that table. So where does the different behaviour come from?

2. Delimiting an entry with the Delimit button only changes the end date. On this forum I've read that the button is supposed to split the entry into two entries.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Bonus Assignment Statement-Adding Person ID and Prefe...
entry.content:
Hi @rkumar1947 

I had a custom statements/reporting expert look at your question and he confirms that your suggestion is the only way to pull up this information into Bonus Assignment Statement. 

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Posting Journal Entries with alternative accounts in...
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Manager Form Eligibility Rules - Variable Pay Management
entry.content: @amuehlbauer Did you apply the rule then relaunch a worksheet or run an update all worksheets job ? If the worksheet already existed applying the rule isn't enough. I know it sounds obvious if you are an expert of Variable Pay but just checking before I investigate a bit more because a week ago I spent 2 hours working on something reported on this forum (inactive employees aren't being retrieved) when the system admin simply hadn't unchecked the "Active users only" checkbox 🙂 after doing that it turned out they showed up hence there was never any issue to begin with. Thanks!

entry.title: After registering with universal ID, I am unable to login to my older profile and access my blog
entry.content:
HI,

I have posted a blog back in 2023 using my S user id. I recently created a universal ID and linked it to my S user id. However, now I am not able to access my blog post.

This new account

1. Doesn't show my blog post in "My posts". 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/how-to-optimize-the-technical-monitoring-of-application-in-solution-manager/ba-p/13549991

2. Doesn't allow me to post any blog at all.

How can I get my access back?

Please help!.

Thanks,

Aditya.

entry.title: Quality notification partner SAP CLOUD PUBLIC EDITION
entry.content:
I am creating this question because we found that for sap public cloud, it is not possible to modify the partner determination procedures form the CBC, by the contraty, it is just possible to access in diplay mode.

However, for a project, My client has requested to insert a department/group as responsible partner and not just a sap user, and to all the people inside that department/group, send the notification or task. They need to insert as a partner in a quality notification, each department of their company, not just a user but the whole department. I have try with the manage teams and responsabilities app, but we did not find a solution. The last an unique way that I know it could work is doing it from the customizing, as in the private and in the on-premise solution it is possible to do it, and this request would be solved doing this process.

But in the public cloud, I am not allowed to implement a new partner determination procedure in which I want to implement the partner function AB (department responsible) inside the quality notifications.

I need your help in order to understand if there is another workaround for this problem in which I can insert a department of the company inside a notification as a partner.

Maybe I found a way to solve this, using the position responsible ST type of partner. The problem is that there is no data available in the search help. There is no data for position responsible and I can not find a way to insert data for this type of partner. Do you know how to do it? Could you help me with this?

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - (how-to) deploy and use pgAdmin web version
entry.content:
Hello Antonio 

All of sudden my admin user login is not working for PostgreSQL web application with the password I setup initially during docker app deployment (BTP Cloud Foundry). After I have entered password in pgAdmin web browser and select login, again it is retrieving the login screen and no error message appearing. It looks like my admin user is locked. 

Can you suggest how do I unlock / reset password for Admin user. 

Thanks

Delli

entry.title: Partial invoicing with item category TAD service
entry.content:
Hello,

My client wants to create a partial sales invoice (F2) from a sales order without delivery.

The item category that we are using is TAD for service.

How can i put in place this process? 

I have changed the billing relevance of the item category to K.

In the header of the copy control, I have maintained a copy requirement of "001" and item leve for copying requirement i have maintained "002" and data VBRK/VBRP "001". 

When i tried to create the invoice, the system is throwing me this message:

Error Log

XXXXX 000010 The item is not relevant for billing.

Can you please help me?

@Lakshmipathi

@Jignesh_Mehta

entry.title: Re: Manager Form Eligibility Rules - Variable Pay Management
entry.content:
@amuehlbauer Also, you may want to check that you use the right date format(s) in your rule, for example since I am based in the US when I run an employee export, even though the date that was loaded for an employee in France was loaded as DD/MM/YYYY in Excel I see it as M/D/YYYY. You may want to add an OR in your condition to take into account both date formats and try that as a fix. I open CSV files exported from SuccessFactors in Notepad++ (free light code editor) when I want to check that quickly.

 

 

 

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Enhancements - February 2025
entry.content: Thank you @AnnePetteroe  and @Marian_Zeis . Will keep an eye on the ideation board for future suggestions.
